name: Instagram Post Reel from URL
description: Verifies the correct Instagram account is active, downloads a video from a URL to device storage, then uploads it as a Reel with a caption.

params.username: The Instagram username that should be active
params.videoUrl: Direct URL to the video file to download
params.caption: Caption text for the Reel

steps.goal: Open Instagram. Check which account is currently logged in by looking at the profile tab or the username displayed at the top. If the active account is 'your_username', proceed (mark done). If a different account is active, tap the username at the top to open the account switcher, then select 'your_username'. If 'your_username' is not listed, mark as failed.

Use the 'download' action to download the video file from URL https://example.com/video.mp4 to the device. Use: {"action": "download", "url": "https://example.com/video.mp4", "text": "reel_video.mp4"}. After the download action completes successfully, mark as done. If it fails, mark as failed. Do NOT open a browser — use the download action directly.

Open Instagram. Tap the '+' (create/new post) button at the bottom center of the screen. Select 'Reel' from the options (not Story or Post). On the media picker screen, look for the recently downloaded video — it should be the most recent item in the gallery. If you don't see it, check the 'Downloads' folder or 'Recent' tab. Select the video. On the trim/edit screen, tap 'Next' or the arrow to proceed. On the caption screen, type the caption: 'Your caption here'. Tap 'Share' or 'Post' to publish the Reel. Wait for the upload to complete.
